mechanism_narrative: A sustained two-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing activity that translates into higher near-term earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following two-consecutive-month INDPRO increases are not positive in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances fail to produce negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the mean forward return is not statistically negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a three-month window reveal broad-based demand collapse that amplifies recession probabilities beyond either indicator alone, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline over prior 3 months
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST changes fails to add incremental predictive power (F-test p > 0.05) relative to single-factor models over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy, month-over-month contractions in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate that elevated interest rates are cracking labor market resilience, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings recession fears mount.
failure_condition: During regimes where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) over a one-month window captures manufacturing momentum that rapidly translates into cyclical equity performance, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive INDPRO changes fail to coincide with positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a rolling 2-year window, or the information coefficient falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5%, additional tightening sharply increases hard-landing probabilities and compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Episodes with FEDFUNDS above 5% are associated with positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of trading months, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) growth coinciding with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand without cost-push wage pressure, supporting corporate margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Jointly favorable RSAFS and UNRATE months do not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or a regression interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly surprise in real GDP (GDPC1) relative to trend signals persistent aggregate demand strength that supports earnings forecasts over the subsequent quarter, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises fail to correlate with positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 5-year rolling window, or the hit rate falls below 55%.
